title: The export-control order that shut down Fable is now becoming a shared AI rulebook - TCR 06/20/26
description:
Washington softened its export order against Anthropic from a shutdown into co-writing the rules for grading AI security flaws, the administration announced an Apple-Intel deal to build chips in the US as Intel's stock hit a record and Amazon disclosed talks to sell its Trainium chips to rivals, and AlphaFold's Nobel laureate John Jumper left Google DeepMind for Anthropic.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-june-20-2026/

0:00 Cold Open
0:44 Anthropic export standoff shifts to rule-writing
3:57 Apple-Intel deal and Amazon's custom silicon
6:26 Claude runs robotics 20x faster
9:16 John Jumper leaves DeepMind for Anthropic
11:44 Subquadratic's sparse-attention SubQ model
14:28 Synthesis and Closing

This episode covers the Anthropic export standoff shifting to joint rule-writing, the Apple-Intel chip deal and Amazon's custom-silicon push, Claude Opus 4.7 running robotics tasks 20x faster than the fastest human team, John Jumper's move to Anthropic, and Miami startup Subquadratic's sparse-attention SubQ model.

One week pulled in the same direction from five places: who holds the levers over the substrate of the intelligence era, and how long any single hand can keep its grip. The Anthropic order softened from a kill switch into a shared rulebook, even as Glasswing testers like Cisco and Dragos kept Mythos Preview access throughout. Moves to pair Apple with Intel and to sell Amazon's accelerators into rival data centers both aim at loosening the single-foundry, single-vendor compute layer. Claude ran a quadruped's tasks on its own roughly 20 times faster than the fastest human team a year earlier, a leap Anthropic credits to general scaling rather than robotics engineering. AlphaFold's John Jumper carried Nobel-winning AI-for-science expertise across an institutional boundary to Anthropic. And a Miami startup's independently benchmarked SubQ model points at frontier-grade capability without ever-larger compute budgets. The grip is loosening everywhere at once.
